title: List of National Treasures of Japan (writings: others)
rows: Segment of the Sutra of the Wise and Foolish ( 賢愚経残巻 , Kengukyō zankan ) , Yamato edition
Emperor Shomu attributed to Emperor Shōmu

Chapters 8 ( Vajra , the Daughter of King Prasenajit ) , 9 ( Golden Wealth ) , 10 ( Heavenly Flowers ) , 11 ( Heavenly Jewels ) , and the final lines of Chapter 48 ( Upagupta ) of the Sutra of the Wise and Foolish , or Sutra of the Karma of the Wise and Foolish ; total of 262 lines with eleven to fourteen characters per line ; also known as Great Shōmu ( 大聖武 , ōshōmu ) after Emperor Shōmu ; originally kept at Tōdai-ji in Yamato
One handscroll , ink on paper , 25.7 cm × 696.9 cm ( 10.1 in × 274.4 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo
Segment of the Sutra of the Wise and Foolish ( 賢愚経残巻 , Kengukyō zankan )
Emperor Shomu attributed to Emperor Shōmu
Volumes 1 ( 419 lines ) , 2 ( 149 lines ) , 3 ( 18 lines ) ; also known as Great Shōmu ( 大聖武 , ōshōmu ) after Emperor Shōmu
Tokyo Tokyo Maeda Ikutokukai Maeda Ikutokukai , Tokyo
Segment of the Sutra of the Wise and Foolish ( 賢愚経残巻 , Kengukyō zankan )
Emperor Shomu attributed to Emperor Shōmu
Volumes 1 ( 461 lines ) , 2 ( 503 lines ) ; also known as Great Shōmu ( 大聖武 , ōshōmu ) after Emperor Shōmu ; formerly in the possession of Kaidan-in , Tōdai-ji
Two handscrolls , ink on paper , 27.5 cm × 1,200 cm ( 10.8 in × 472.4 in )
Hyogo Kobe Hakutsuru Fine Art Museum Hakutsuru Fine Art Museum , Kobe , Hyōgo
Lotus Sutra , Chapter on Expedient Means ( 法華経方便品 , Hokekyō hōbenbon )
Minamoto Toshifusa attributed to Minamoto Toshifusa ( 源俊房 ) by Shōkadō Shōjō in a postscript from 1625

28 lines per page ; also known as Chikubushima Sutra ( 竹生島経 ) as the scroll is in possession of Hōgon-ji on Chikubu Island ; paper decorated with gold and silver underdrawings of butterflies , birds , flowering plants , imaginary Buddhist flowers , and clouds ; the introductory chapter of the same work , located at Hōgon-ji , has been designated as a National Treasure
One handscroll , ink on decorated paper , 29.6 cm × 528.5 cm ( 11.7 in × 208.1 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo
Lotus Sutra in minute characters ( 細字法華経 , saiji hokekyō )
Li Yuanhui transcription by Li Yuanhui ( 李元恵 , rigenkei )
39 pages of 56 ruled lines with 32 characters per line ; also known as Honored Companion Sutra ( 御同朋経 , godōbōkyō ) ; handed down at Hōryū-ji
One handscroll , ink on paper ; 25.7 cm × 2,150 cm ( 10.1 in × 846.5 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Tokyo National Museum Tokyo National Museum , Tokyo
Commentary on the Vimalakirti Sutra ( 浄名玄論 , jōmyō genron )
Oldest extant text using the Japanese dating system ; 20 to 40 characters per line ; originally in the possession of Tōdai-ji
Eight handscrolls , ink on paper , height : 27.8-28.0 cm ( 10.9-11.0 in ) , length : 296.0-1,092.0 cm ( 116.5-429.9 in )
Kyoto Kyoto Kyoto National Museum Kyoto National Museum , Kyoto
Mahāratnakūṭa Sūtra on deep blue paper ( 紺紙金字大宝積経 , konshikinji taihō shakukyō ) vol . 32 , Goryeo Tripiṭaka with gilt letters ( 高麗国金字大蔵経 , kōraikoku kinji daizōkyō )

Earliest copied sutra of the Goryeo dynasty and only surviving volume of a set of complete Buddhist scriptures , offered by Queen Heonae and Kim Chi-yang . The cover is decorated with a hōsōge flower pattern and the frontispiece with three bodhisattvas scattering flower offerings , both in silver paint
One handscroll , gilt letters on deep blue paper , 29.1 cm × 881.2 cm ( 11.5 in × 346.9 in )
Kyoto Kyoto Kyoto National Museum custody of Kyoto National Museum , Kyoto , Kyoto ; owned by National Institutes for Cultural Heritage ( 独立行政法人国立文化財機構 )
Segment of the Sutra of the Incantation of the one thousand armed , one thousand eyed Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva ( 千手千眼陀羅尼経残巻 , senju sengen daranikyō zankan )

Only extant portion of one thousand copies of the Senju sengen daranikyō made by Genbō ; mentioned in the Essential Records of Tōdai-ji ( 東大寺要録 , tōdaiji yōroku ) ; total of 109 lines ; beginning of scroll is lost
One handscroll , ink on paper , 25.5 cm × 246.0 cm ( 10.0 in × 96.9 in )
Kyoto Kyoto Kyoto National Museum Kyoto National Museum , Kyoto
Golden Light of the Most Victorious Kings Sutra ( 紫紙金字金光明最勝王経 , shishikinji konkōmyō saishōōkyō )
One of the sutras enshrined in the state-sponsored Temples for the Protection of the State by the Golden Light ( of the ) Four Heavenly Kings ; said to have been enshrined in Bingo Province
0741 Nara period , 8th century , Tenpyō era after 741
Ten handscrolls , gilt letters on violet paper , 26.4 cm × 841.1 cm ( 10.4 in × 331.1 in ) ( vol . 1 )
Nara Nara Nara National Museum Nara National Museum , Nara , Nara
Dhāraṇī of the Adamantine Place ( 金剛場陀羅尼経 , Kongō Jōdaranikyō )
Horin transcription by the priest Hōrin
Tokyo Tokyo Agency for Cultural Affairs Agency for Cultural Affairs , Tokyo
Konkōmyō Saishōō Sutra with gilt letters ( 紫紙金字金光明最勝王経 , shishikinji konkōmyō saishōōkyō )
One of the sutras enshrined in the state-sponsored Temples for the Protection of the State by the Golden Light ( of the ) Four Heavenly Kings founded by Emperor Shōmu
Wakayama Koya Reihokan Reihōkan ( owned by Ryūkō-in ( 龍光院 ) ) , Kōya , Wakayama
Mahavairocana Sutra ( 大毘盧遮那成仏神変加持経 , Daibirushana jōbutsu jinpen kajikyō )
Seven handscrolls , width : 27.6 cm ( 10.9 in ) , length : 56.0-1,386.1 cm ( 22.0-545.7 in )
Nara Nara Saidaiji Saidai-ji , Nara , Nara
Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra ( 大般若経 , Daihannya-kyō ) or Yakushi-ji Sutra ( 薬師寺経 , Yakushiji-kyō )
Formerly in the possession of Yakushi-ji
387 handscrolls , ink on paper , height : 27.3 cm ( 10.7 in )
Osaka Osaka Fujita Art Museum Fujita Art Museum , Osaka
Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra ( 大般若経 , Daihannya-kyō )
Made on request of Prince Nagaya praying for the deceased Emperor Mommu
Shiga Koka Jomyoji Jōmyō-ji ( 常明寺 ) , Kōka , Shiga
Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra ( 大般若経 , Daihannya-kyō )
Made on request of Prince Nagaya praying for the deceased Emperor Mommu ; oldest extant manuscript of the Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra
Shiga Koka Taiheiji Taihei-ji ( 太平寺 ) , Kōka , Shiga
Konkōmyō Saishōō Sutra ( 金光明最勝王経 , Konkōmyō Saishōōkyō )
Ten handscrolls , 32.4 cm × 803.0 cm ( 12.8 in × 316.1 in )
Nara Nara Saidaiji Saidai-ji , Nara , Nara
Konpon hyakuichi konma ( 根本百一羯磨 ) vol . 6
Transcription of a Chinese translation from 703 , 12-13 characters per line
One handscroll , ink on paper , 27.4 cm × 1,164.9 cm ( 10.8 in × 458.6 in )
Tokyo Tokyo Nezu Art Museum Nezu Art Museum , Tokyo
Sutra of the Wise and Foolish ( 賢愚経 , kengukyō ) vol . 15
Nara Nara Todaiji Tōdai-ji , Nara , Nara
Lotus Sutra in large characters ( 大字法華経 , daiji hokekyō )
Wakayama Koya Reihokan Reihōkan ( owned by Ryūkō-in ( 龍光院 ) ) , Kōya , Wakayama
Fukū Kenjaku Shinpen Shingon Sutra ( 不空羂索神変真言経 , fukū kenjaku shinpen shingonkyō )
Wakayama Koya Reihokan Reihōkan ( owned by Sanbō-in ( 三宝院 ) ) , Kōya , Wakayama

description: This table provides a list of National Treasures of Japan categorized under writings, specifically focusing on various manuscripts and documents. It includes details such as the name of the item, associated authors, remarks about the content or significance, the date of origin, the format of the writing, and its current location. The table serves as a reference for historical and cultural artifacts of significant importance in Japan.
